nodes.label: ∅ | be | valley
nodes.sentence: Far away from here, there was once a beautiful valley covered in trees, surrounded by tall mountains.

nodes.label: river | flow | valley
nodes.sentence: A powerful river flowed through this valley, watering all the different trees and plants that grew there.

nodes.label: animal | live | valley
nodes.sentence: Many animals lived in this peaceful valley—rabbits, birds, squirrels, and deer.

nodes.label: they | live | ∅
nodes.sentence: They all lived happily because no wolves or lions lived there to hunt them.

nodes.label: ∅ | climb | ∅
nodes.sentence: One day, however, a wolf climbed down from the mountains and entered the valley.

nodes.label: he | begin | ∅
nodes.sentence: As soon as he arrived, he began chasing the helpless animals, eating them one by one.

nodes.label: animal | manage | ∅
nodes.sentence: Only rarely did any animal manage to run away unharmed, but all the others feared it would soon be their turn.

nodes.label: animal | turn | owl
nodes.sentence: Worried, the animals turned to the wise old owl and asked him to find a way to get rid of the wolf.

nodes.label: owl | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: The owl said there was no way to fight the wolf; his sharp teeth and powerful paws were stronger than any other animal in the valley.

nodes.label: owl | advise | they
nodes.sentence: The owl advised them to learn to live with the wolf.

nodes.label: they | make | plan
nodes.sentence: The animals protested they couldn’t live in constant fear, so they made a desperate plan: each day, one animal would be chosen to go to the wolf and be eaten.

nodes.label: rest | live | peace
nodes.sentence: That way, the rest could live in peace, knowing the wolf had already had his meal for the day.

nodes.label: wolf | agree | plan
nodes.sentence: The wolf, tired of the chase and pleased with the idea of food coming to him, agreed to this plan without hesitation.

nodes.label: animal | gather | ∅
nodes.sentence: The next morning, the animals gathered and decided that the little rabbit—the smallest and weakest in the valley—would be the one to be given to the wolf.

nodes.label: rabbit | try | ∅
nodes.sentence: Terrified, the rabbit first tried to run away but soon realized there was nowhere to hide.

nodes.label: he | think | ∅
nodes.sentence: He thought about fighting but knew the wolf was far too strong.

nodes.label: he | walk | den
nodes.sentence: So he quietly walked to the wolf’s den and called out, “Oh wolf!

nodes.label: ∅ | come | ∅
nodes.sentence: Come out of your lair, for I am your supper today.”

nodes.label: wolf | come | ∅
nodes.sentence: The wolf came out and sniffed the rabbit hungrily.

nodes.label: wolf | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: “What a delicious little meal you will make!” said the wolf.

nodes.label: i | believe | luck
nodes.sentence:
“I can’t believe my luck to find a valley where animals bring themselves to me.”

“It’s true,” the rabbit sighed.

nodes.label: i | come | foot
nodes.sentence: “I came here on my own four feet because I know I can’t escape my fate.

nodes.label: scary | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Besides, a mighty wolf like you—though not the scariest or strongest in this valley—is still hard to escape.”

nodes.label: wolf | stun | ∅
nodes.sentence: The vain wolf was stunned.

nodes.label: i | ’m | scary
nodes.sentence: I’m not the scariest or strongest?

nodes.label: i | ’m | wolf
nodes.sentence: I’m the only wolf here!

nodes.label: ∅ | ’ | wolf
nodes.sentence: There’s no other wolf in this valley!”

nodes.label: rabbit | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: “You don’t know about the other wolf,” said the rabbit.

nodes.label: you | eat | i
nodes.sentence: “Anyway, you should eat me now, because even if I escape you, no animal could escape the other, scarier wolf.”

nodes.label: rabbit | try | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then the rabbit tried to climb into the wolf’s mouth.

nodes.label: wolf | offend | ∅
nodes.sentence: The wolf was offended and shook the rabbit out.

nodes.label: he | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: “Take me to this other wolf,” he said, “and I will spare you for today, tasty morsel.

nodes.label: ∅ | show | i
nodes.sentence: Show me where this wolf lives who thinks he’s better than me.”

nodes.label: rabbit | sigh | ∅
nodes.sentence: The rabbit sighed and said, “What does it matter?

nodes.sentence: In the end, I will be eaten by a wolf—whether it’s you or the other with bigger teeth and stronger legs.

Follow me.”

“Hmph!” said the wolf.

nodes.label: we | see | ∅
nodes.sentence: “We’ll see who’s stronger.

nodes.label: ∅ | lead | ∅
nodes.sentence: Lead the way!”

nodes.label: wolf | follow | rabbit
nodes.sentence: The wolf followed the rabbit until they reached an old, abandoned well.

nodes.label: ∅ | be | lair
nodes.sentence: “There,” said the rabbit, “is the lair of the other wolf, who is stronger and fiercer than you.

nodes.label: ∅ | look | well
nodes.sentence: Just look down into the well and you’ll see him resting after his last meal.”

nodes.label: wolf | jump | edge
nodes.sentence: The wolf jumped up on the edge of the well and peered down into the darkness.

nodes.sentence: “I don’t see anything; it’s too dark!”

nodes.sentence: he said.

nodes.label: rabbit | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: “You have to look closer,” the rabbit said.

nodes.label: ∅ | put | head
nodes.sentence: “Put your whole head down into the well and you’ll see him looking back.”

nodes.label: wolf | bent | ∅
nodes.sentence: So the wolf bent over and stuck his head into the well.

nodes.label: eye | adjust | moment
nodes.sentence: After a moment, his eyes adjusted and he saw his own reflection in the water below, as if another wolf was staring back.

nodes.label: i | see | you
nodes.sentence: Now I see you, you coward!”

nodes.label: ∅ | come | ∅

nodes.sentence: The rabbit watched the wolf argue with himself and said, “I don’t think he’s coming out.

nodes.sentence: But because he could not swim, he never came out, and the valley was freed from the evil wolf—all thanks to a small, clever rabbit.
